\end{table}

\paragraph{Key Findings}

\begin{itemize}
    \item \textbf{Low-frequency token improvement}: For rare tokens with fewer than 16 occurrences, GRPO improves the 0.6B model from 17.93\% to 26.55\% (relative +48.1\%), while the 8B model improves from 25.23\% to 26.21\% (relative +3.9\%). This indicates that GRPO can improve rare-token prediction in TCM translation.
    \item \textbf{Mid-frequency token translation}: For mid-frequency tokens with 16--512 occurrences, the 0.6B GRPO model reaches 35.50\% (relative +33.8\% over SFT), and the 8B GRPO model reaches 38.84\% (relative +2.2\% over SFT).
    \item \textbf{Frequency--accuracy relationship}: Accuracy increases as token frequency rises. This trend is consistent with causal language modeling, where high-frequency tokens are observed more often and are easier to learn~\cite{radford2018improving}.
    \item \textbf{Rare-token challenge}: Despite SFT and GRPO improvements, extremely low-frequency tokens (<4 occurrences) remain difficult, with substantially lower accuracy than high-frequency tokens. This aligns with core MT challenges in the LLM era reported by Pang et al.~\cite{pang2024mtchallenges}.
\end{itemize}
